source.title: Activation of the opioidergic descending pain control system underlies placebo analgesia

confidence_rationale: This is the key human fMRI + pharmacological dissection paper establishing that top-down cortical expectation (rACC) activates the PAG-RVM descending inhibitory axis via opioid mechanisms. Naloxone abolishes both rACC-PAG coupling and the behavioural placebo effect, providing a clean double dissociation. Directly supports the mechanism proposed in SD-021. Confidence is high but not maximal because placebo is not identical to voluntary goal-directed commitment -- it is belief/expectation-based, not yet motor-commitment-gated.
failure_signatures: If naloxone's effect is non-specific (e.g., anxiolytic rather than opioidergic), the descending modulation interpretation weakens.
If placebo analgesia and commitment-gated analgesia use different cortical origins (rACC vs. pgACC/dACC), the direct mapping to SD-021 would need revision.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Naloxone abolished placebo-induced rACC-PAG coupling, brainstem descending control activation (PAG, RVM, hypothalamus), and behavioural placebo analgesia -- demonstrating that opioidergic rACC-PAG connectivity is the mechanism by which top-down expectation gates sensory pain suppression.
mapping.ree_translation: In REE terms: the rACC/pgACC is the cortical origin of the commitment-gated precision-downweighting signal on z_harm_s. When E3 is committed (beta_gate elevated, MECH-090) and trajectory passes through expected harm, rACC/pgACC sends opioidergic signal to PAG, which recruits RVM off-cells to suppress dorsal horn nociceptive relay -- reducing z_harm_s PE precision. This is the mechanism SD-021 proposes to implement computationally.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Placebo analgesia is expectation-based, not strictly commitment-based in the motor sense. REE's commitment gate (MECH-090 beta oscillation) is a stronger condition than placebo expectation. SD-021 requires that committed E3 trajectory (not merely expected pain reduction) drives this pathway. The mapping is strong for the mechanism but the trigger condition is not identical.
mapping.source_context: Healthy human volunteers; pharmacological fMRI with naloxone vs. saline; thermal pain stimuli; placebo cream paradigm.

confidence_components.notes: High source quality -- human fMRI, pharmacological causal test, published in Neuron, highly replicated. Mapping fidelity is good because the mechanism (rACC-PAG opioid coupling = top-down sensory gating) maps cleanly to SD-021. Transfer risk is low -- same species, same circuit. The main uncertainty is that placebo expectation is not the same as committed motor trajectory; it is a weaker version of the top-down drive. This reduces mapping fidelity slightly but does not undermine the core circuit claim.
